Establish plantilla positions in accordance with national guidelines, comprising a DRRM Officer to be assisted by three staff members;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Engineer, observe the following:
Fast track the submission of contracts with complete set of supporting documents to facilitate the immediate conduct of the auditorial and technical review of the projects; and
For completed projects, submit the complete set of documents required in the same COA circular for technical inspection.

We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Budget Officer, submit a justification for the deficiency in the LCPC allocation for 2024.  Moving forward, the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and/or the Local Sanggunian, is urged to observe the mandatory budgetary requirements for the LCPC to enhance and improve the Municipality’s services related to child welfare and protection.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Budget Officer and Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer:
Comply with the requirement to appropriate in its annual budget at least five per cent (5%) of its estimated revenue from regular sources for LDRRMF, pursuant to the requirements of RA No. 10121 and JMC No. 2013-1; and
Appropriate the under-allocation of ₱4,073,255.23 to additional disaster risk-related PPAs in the LDRRM budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the GSO, prioritize the registration, transfer, and titling of these parcels of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Barili to ensure ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-386. In the alternative, we recommended for the creation of Land Titling Committee to intensify/expedite the titling efforts of the municipality.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, establish ways to improve the cash position such as, but not limited to, efficient revenue collection and adoption of effective austerity measures.

We also recommended that Management, through the Municipal Budget Officer, review, prioritize and update the programs, projects and activities taking into consideration the total cash available for their implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) comply with the mandatory posting of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020. Henceforth, we recommended that the BAC always be updated with the issuances and advisories of the GPPB by visiting their website regularly to avoid any more non-compliance in the future.
observations.managementAction: In view of the urgent necessity of the procurement covered substantially under the Bayanihan Act, the LGU commenced the procurement even without the approved APP. The procurement is anchored on the certification from the budget officer showing that there is a budget for the purpose. The LGU will comply, with the APP approved thereafter, be posted on the PE’s website and submit it to the GPPB or this will be posted on the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.
observations.reason: Posting requirements were complied substantially in the current year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management stop providing for a specific appropriation in the annual budget as funding source for payment of honoraria of government personnel involved in government procurement. Instead, the end of year, the sanggunian shall pass a supplemental budget for payment of such honoraria funded from the savings defined under Section 3.2 of DBM BC No. 2007-3.
We also recommended that the members of the BAC, the BAC Secretariat and the TWG be made to explain the amount of honoraria they each received since such expenditures were considered irregular.
observations.managementAction: The Annual Budget for CY 2021 was already been reviewed and approved by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan dated February 23, 2021 which part of the said budget is the appropriation for BAC Honoraria. The recommendation to stop providing for specific appropriation in the annual budget for payment of honoraria of government personnel involved in the procurement process cannot be complied yet. However, the management will refrain from disbursing any amount from said appropriation. Rest assured that the management will no longer provide such appropriation in the next budget year.
observations.reason: Considered as fully implemented by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to submit justification or any legal basis as to utilization of FY 2019 savings, instead of the savings or any available appropriation from FY 2020 local government funds for payment of COVID-19 hazard pay allowance as stated on the circular. We also recommended that management submit the computation of savings from FY 2019 with complete description of its expense class, to enable the Audit Team to conduct further evaluation as to the over-all propriety of disbursements relative to COVID-19 Hazard Pay.
observations.managementAction:
A copy of the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations for the year ended December 31, 2019 for Personal Services, MOOE and Capital Outlay was submitted.
The total amounts of unobligated balances for Personal Services and MOOE are ₱15,197,474.47 and ₱42,170,211.71 respectively. The total amounts of unobligated balances for Personal Services and MOOE are ₱15,197,474.47 and ₱42,170,211.71 respectively, thus of the above cited amount, ₱1,151,572.64 was used to fund for the Supplemental Budget 1-2020 for COVID-19 Hazard Pay of Health and Disaster Risk Management Workers.
observations.reason: Substantially complied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor, as the head of the agency, to direct the Municipal Treasurer to take positive action on the following:
1. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Cashbooks balances with the accounting records in a regular basis in order to timely effect the necessary corrections and consistently harmonize the balances of both records; and
2. Generate computerized copies (excel format) of the Monthly Report of Collections and Deposits or Abstract of Collections from CY 2013 up to the present and furnish the team with the softcopies of the same in order to facilitate our verification of the discrepancies noted in the Municipal Accountant’s reconciliation reports; and
3. Lastly, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to create a single monthly reconciliation report for the Cash in Local Treasury account or prepare the Office of the Treasurer’s version of reconciliation report so that we could use the same along with the report of the Municipal Accountant in establishing for the verified amounts of accountability.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer is now regularly coordinating with the Municipal Accountant in timely effecting the necessary corrections so that the cashbook and general ledger balances are harmonized every month.
The cashbooks and the general ledgers are now finally reconciled. The reconciliation report will be submitted by the Municipal Accountant. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer is very grateful to the COA Audit Team in guiding the LGU in achieving good financial recording as good partners in government service.
observations.reason: The cashbooks and the general ledgers are now finally reconciled. The reconciliation report will be submitted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor, as the head of the agency, to direct the Municipal Treasurer to take positive action on the following:
1. Submit an explanation or reconciliation report on the discrepancy noted between the cashbook balances and the actual cash count of the audit team;
2. Ensure that all collections and deposits are recorded daily on the cashbooks and corrections or any adjustments, if necessary, are timely effected; and
3. Lastly, insert a column for the daily ending (running) balance in the cashbooks to reconcile the same with the total amount of cash on hand at the end of each day.
observations.managementAction: The discrepancy noted in the cashbook and the actual cash count has been reconciled. The discrepancy was due to the official leave of absence of the Liquidating Officer during the time of audit. The Municipal Treasurer ensures now that all collections and deposits are recorded daily on the cashbooks effectively making the corrections and adjustments daily. The ending balances of the cashbooks are now reflected in order to achieve and establish the total amount of cash on hand daily.
observations.reason: Complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their balances. Further, instruct the Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer and other Section Chiefs to coordinate closely with the Municipal Accountant regarding the nature and other details of various property and equipment used by the respective offices.

observations.reason: The result of the physical count provided in the submitted RPCPPE was reconciled with the records of the Municipal Accountant. However, there were still deficiencies noted in the preparation of such report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should instruct the Municipal
   Accountant to reconcile their balances. Further, instruct  the  Municipal
   Treasurer/Property Officer and other Section Chiefs to coordinate closely
   with the Municipal Accountant regarding the nature and other  details  of
   various property and equipment used by the respective offices.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify  the  Loans
   Payable account into current  and  non-current  portion  for  the  proper
   presentation of the account in the Statement of Financial Position.


        We also  recommended  to  submit  the  documents  relative  to  the
   agency’s loan pertaining to the construction of auction market.

We recommended that  Management  (a)  require  the  GSO  and  other
   custodians of supplies to undertake the actual physical  inventory  every
   semester and to submit inventory reports to the Auditor; and  (b)  enjoin
   all  stock  custodians  and the Accounting Office to perform the
   reconciliation of the inventories  with  the  recorded  balances  in  the
   books.

We recommended and Management agreed that they will now require the
   Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile entries in  the  Cashbook
   and General Ledger at least on a quarterly basis.


        We also recommended to submit a reconciliation as  regards  to  the
   discrepancy of year-end  balances  and  effect  the  necessary  adjusting
   journal entries to come up with the correct and reconciled balance of the
   Cash Local Treasury account.

We recommended and Management agreed that those accounts which  can
   no longer be traced by them may request for write-off of  their  accounts
   duly supported with at least  three  (3)  demand  letters  and  proof  of
   separation/death and other valid documents supporting their best  efforts
   for collection.


        We also recommended to  conduct  regular  monitoring  and  periodic
   analysis of all the receivables to ensure that these are  collected  when
   these become due and demandable in pursuant to  existing  COA  rules  and
   regulations.

We recommended that Management shall see to it that the conduct  of
   detailed engineering in various  infrastructure  projects  shall  at  all
   times be carried out in accordance with the provisions of RA 9184 and its
   2016 Revised IRR.


        We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer immediately  comply
   with the requirements of the TITS office in order to facilitate  contract
   review and for the audit team to assess the propriety and  reasonableness
   of the amount paid.

We recommended that Management and  the  member  of  the  Bids  and
   Awards Committee (BAC) submit  explanation/justification  for  the  above
   violations at the same time provide proof that  the  government  was  not
   prejudiced in any way in order to avoid disallowance.


        We also recommended that Management and the BAC members observe the
   basic law on procurement in their future procurement activities.


        Lastly, we  recommended  that  the  lacking  documents  be  submitted
   by Management to the Auditors for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Management refund the total  excess  amount
   of the Contract and COA Cost estimates.

It is recommended and Management agreed that the  noted  deficiency
   be corrected and the required documents be submitted immediately to avoid
   disallowance. Thereafter, all the officers involved in the processing  of
   the claim should see to it that the supporting papers  are  complete  and
   proper, before payment is made.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant should determine the correct and legitimate payable accounts which are supported with complete documentation and revert to the unappropriated surplus those accounts which have been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claim have been filed or which are not covered by perfected contracts on record. Henceforth, accounts payable should be recognized in the books only when goods and services are accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received to insure that only legitimate liabilities (supported with complete documentary evidence) are recorded.

Subsidiary ledgers per creditor account should be maintained to facilitate monitoring of account balances and avoid difficulties in application of payments made.

We recommend that the municipality ensure that the  20% development funds be optimally utilized to help attain desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and see to it that the programs projects funded out of the funds are in accordance with the provisions of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum provisions of Joint Circular 1-11 for the programming for development project. We recommend that management submit a valid justification on the discrepancies in the General and Specific Conditions of the Contract prepared by the Municipality of six (6) projects totaling P 22,436,255.35 specifically on the Delivery Period/Contract Time of the project was not in consonance with the Bid Notice Abstract as posted in the PHILGEPS and without any supplemental bid/bulletin.
Likewise, the Bids and Awards Committee should also submit a valid justification on recommending the award to a bidder that was not a responsive bidder, since the duration time was not followed.
Lastly, if there is no valid justifiable reason for the discrepancy, appropriate liquidated damages should be imposed on the contractors for the delayed completion of the projects.

We recommend the management to direct the VSMMC DOH Botica to cause the immediate delivery of the drugs and medicines presented on Annex A.
We recommend further to revisit the underlying standards and principles in asset recognition and familiarize more the accounts provided under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that the person preparing the Job Order Contracts specify the duties and responsibilities of each of the Job Order personnel and require thereof the submission of individual quantified accomplishment report to form part of the supporting papers in the claim of their wages.

We recommend that:

The municipal engineer and other officials concerned be made to explain their failure to monitor the completion of the projects, the seeming lack of planning in the prosecution of the projects, their lack of initiative to recommend alternative courses of action as provided under the IRR of RA 9184.

Liquidated damages be computed, imposed and collected on the payment for the construction beyond the contract expiry date.

Henceforth, to adequately plan and supervise the implementation of infrastructure projects to ensure that these are completed as scheduled.
